channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Oklahoma City to mark 30 years since the bombing that killed 168 people and shook America
item.description: A bomb with a force powerful enough to instantly destroy much of a nine-story building shattered a quiet Oklahoma City morning and sent a shock wave through America.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: El Salvador president responds after Dem visits alleged MS-13 member and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Surprise open governor's seat in Iowa signals competitive GOP primary, glimmer of hope for Democrats
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa looked a lot different in 2010, when Kim Reynolds was first elected to statewide office. Reynolds was the Republican nominee for lieutenant governor on the ticket alongside former Gov. Terry Branstad.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration seeks explosive expansion of nation's immigration detention system
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Amid rural Louisiana's crawfish farms, towering pine trees and cafes serving po'boys, nearly 7,000 people are waiting at immigration detention centers to learn whether they will be expelled from the United States.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge in Baltimore temporarily limits DOGE access to Social Security data
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday imposed new restrictions on billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, limiting its access to Social Security systems that hold personal data on millions of Americans. U.S.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump officials' defiance over Abrego Garcia's deportation is 'shocking,' appeals court says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration’s claim that it can't do anything to free Kilmar Abrego Garcia from an El Salvador prison and return him to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for months: Source
item.description: President Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for several months, a source confirms to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews scrambled to restore power to Puerto Rico on Thursday after a blackout hit the entire island the previous day, affecting the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Luigi Mangione was indicted Thursday on a federal murder charge in the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, a required step as prosecutors work to make good on the Trump administration’s order to seek the death penalty for what it called a “premeditated, cold-blooded assas
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A US man hijacks a small plane in Belize and stabs people. He is fatally shot and plane lands safely
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A U.S. man hijacked a small plane in Belize on Thursday, stabbing two passengers and a pilot, before one of the stabbed passengers fatally shot him, officials in Belize and the United States said. The plane then landed safely.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Pizookie coming to BJ’s Restaurants
item.description: The Broccoli Cheddar Pizookie, slated to debut at BJ’s Restaurant & Brewhouse nationwide, won’t be coming to restaurants after all. Instead, BJ’s will be releasing a Snickers Pizookie. “We've come to our senses.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas press conference in Austin Metcalf killing devolves into chaos over track meet stabbing
item.description: Austin Metcalf's father, Jeff Metcalf, was denied entry to a press conference held by the suspected killer's family, which turned chaotic as the family's spokesman ranted about race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 1 person killed, several injured after school bus flips on South Carolina highway
item.description: Multiple Lexington County, South Carolina, students were taken to the hospital after a school bus flipped on a the highway Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train on Thursday in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Student who shot 4 at Dallas high school was targeting specific student, school police chief says
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student who shot four students when he opened fire at a Dallas high school this week was in and out of the building in less than two minutes and is believed to have been targeting a specific student, the school district police chief said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KTLA 5

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Donald Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Over 150 dogs rescued from 'extremely poor' conditions inside home
item.description: 152 dogs were rescued from a home in Ogden, Utah, where they were reportedly living in "extremely poor and overcrowded" conditions, according to the Ogden Police Department. Lt.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Fox 5

item.title: MLB ump leaves game after foul ball hits him in the face
item.description: Veteran umpire Hunter Wendelstedt instantly hit the ground after he took a direct hit from the line smash off the bat of New York Mets center fielder Tyrone Taylor in the seventh inning.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jan. 6 defendants, allies vexed with Trump admin
item.description: The Jan. 6 defendants granted sweeping clemency by President Trump are growing increasingly vexed with his administration because they don’t believe it’s delivering on his campaign promise of payback for their prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wink Martindale, the genial game show host and an early TV interviewer of Elvis Presley, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, Fox 5

item.title: Lawyer for detained Palestinian activist says client is relieved he hasn't been moved from Vermont
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A Palestinian man arrested at a Vermont immigration office during an interview about finalizing his U.S. citizenship is relieved that he hasn't been moved out of state and thankful for those supporting him, his attorney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: Fewer people crossed state lines to obtain abortions in 2024 than a year earlier, a new survey has found.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University was arrested Monday at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship, his attorneys said.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southern Utah fire chief's trespass charge dismissed after donation, apology letter
item.description: Hurricane Fire Chief Joe Decker's charge was dismissed after he submitted a letter of apology and made a financial donation.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at NC pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9
